chunk_summary: This section outlines a structured tele-nutrition workflow from booking to outcome tracking, emphasizing responsibility handoffs, risk stratification, and registry-based follow-ups for high-risk cases.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise workflow for tele-nutrition services, including booking, data collection, and follow-up.
Learn how to classify cases into risk tiers and define response targets based on clinical thresholds.
Explore the role of registries in tracking outcomes and prioritizing high-risk cases.
Identify key safety-netting measures for high-risk pediatric nutrition cases.
key_facts: The workflow includes six steps: booking, data collection, clinician review, plan delivery, follow-up reminders, and registry updates.
Step 1 involves consent capture, pathway assignment (e.g., undernutrition, obesity, anemia), and guardian authorization for minors.
High-risk cases, such as suspected SAM with danger signs or severe anemia, should default to referral and safety-netting.
Step 5 mandates weekly registry reviews to prioritize overdue high-risk children and track escalation triggers.
Structured plans should be caregiver-usable, combining text and voice notes for low-literacy populations.
reference_quote: A tele-nutrition service should be designed as an operational pipeline with responsibility handoffs and audit points, not as a single video call.
